data.description: Self-distillation starting at 70% training budget using model snapshot as teacher with softened KL loss
Late QAT via STE applied during final 15% of training

reclassifiable.description: QAT weight-snapping started at 70% of training.
Custom SentencePiece BPE tokenizer with vocab size 4096 trained on FineWeb.
Muon-aware QAT with two modes: STE and Gaussian noise, activated late to preserve Muon's momentum subspace
Mixed quantization with int6 per-row on MLP and attention weights, fp16 passthrough for tied embedding, and QAT using a straight-through estimator.
Late QAT with STE threshold 0.15.
Int6-in-int8 container storage with restricted-range zstd compression
Quantization-aware training with delayed start.
SP4096 SentencePiece BPE tokenizer with improved text compression over sp1024.
Per-row int6 quantization with fp16 embedding passthrough and zstd-22 artifact compression.
2% magnitude pruning applied to the model.
Post-training int8 zlib roundtrip evaluation of the serialized model artifact.
late QAT starting at 85% wallclock to avoid always-on STE instability while closing most of the quantization gap
Trained a custom BPE SentencePiece tokenizer on 2 million FineWeb documents using the same normalization and byte fallback settings as the baseline.
10% magnitude pruning before quantisation to create zero runs that compress better
3% magnitude pruning to improve compressibility.
Late quantization-aware training applied after initial training.
MTP auxiliary heads used during training and stripped before export.
Late QAT with fake int6 quantization (STE) when lr_scale < 0.1.
Magnitude pruning zeros out the smallest 3% of weights post-training.
QAT warmup delay before enabling fake quantization
Magnitude pruning of the smallest 3% of weights post-training to improve compression efficiency.
Late QAT with STE int6 fake-quantization when LR scale drops below 0.1.
Multi-token prediction auxiliary heads predicting tokens 2+ steps ahead during training.
Full-run quantization-aware training with STE fake quantization throughout training.
Late QAT with STE int6 applied when LR scale < 0.1
Late QAT applied when learning-rate scale dropped below 0.1.
Tokenizer optimization using SentencePiece BPE with modified splitting settings and longer max token length.
10% magnitude pruning after EMA averaging and before quantization
Baseline-adjacent mixed-precision export sweep on 1xH200 with fp16 embedding passthrough, compared against clean baseline, sink tokens, and fixed MTP variants.
Late QAT enabled when learning-rate scale drops below 0.1.
Adaptive export-time pruning search to choose the smallest pruning ratio that meets an artifact byte budget.
Calibration-driven precision allocator that evaluates candidate tensor promotions after training, SWA, and pruning, then greedily spends bytes under a 16,000,000-byte cap.
Light export-time pruning of values below 0.0025 before final artifact serialization.
Chebyshev GC guard bounding false reclamation to prevent over-aggressive pruning.
Late QAT with threshold 0.25 using STE int6 quantization in the final portion of training.
Late QAT flag for STE int6 fake-quantization in the final 4% of training, though post-analysis says it was constant-folded and had no effect.
Phonetic tokenization exploration using IPA/G2P conversion and SentencePiece BPE on phonetic output
Late QAT enabled during warmdown when LR scale < 0.1.
Late QAT with threshold 0.25 using STE int6 fake-quantization during warmdown.
Late QAT with int6 STE fake-quantization when LR scale drops below 0.15.
QAT during training to eliminate quantization gap at inference.
Superchunk BPE tokenization: a two-phase BPE where first phase learns merges inside chunks, second phase learns cross-chunk merges interleaved by frequency into one merge table.
No pruning: exact 0.0 clamping removed to preserve absolute model density
Late QAT: int6 fake-quantization applied when learning rate scale < 0.15
Self-distillation with EMA teacher, 50 steps, temperature=2.0, alpha=0.7
Soft-Round QAT: differentiable tanh-based rounding replacing STE with alpha annealing from 1 to 16
Early QAT clipping at threshold 0.5 matched to int5 range
Selective ±1 magnitude pruning post-GPTQ to zero least impactful ±1 quantized values until target artifact size
Late QAT trigger before full GPTQ int6 export
Late Quantization Aware Training (QAT) with STE at threshold 0.15
Bank QAT fix implemented directly in GPT.forward() using STE int6 fake-quantization for bank parameters, with torch.compile reset/recompile.
Uses gated attention, value residuals, late QAT thresholding, and local NVMe staging for data/tokenizer to meet the 10-minute training budget.
Earlier late QAT activation to adapt sooner during warmdown.
Byte-level tokenizer with vocab 260 and no BPE.
LSQ-lite QAT hooks with per-row scales for int8 export alignment.
Tokenizer-free raw UTF-8 byte-level modeling with no tokenizer, BPE, or SentencePiece.
Late QAT with STE int6 fake-quantization when LR scale drops below 0.15
Late QAT threshold tuning to extend quantization-aware adaptation time.
Selective ±1 pruning by reconstruction error
Tokenizer optimization via ablation leverage scoring to replace BPE merge tokens with structurally important tokens.
Retokenization of the training corpus using a custom gravity tokenizer built from selected vocabulary.
Late QAT enabled when lr_scale < 0.1.
Used flash-attn when available, but fell back to zlib export because zstandard was missing on the pod.

verbose_examples: Multi-head language model with kernel-based readout heads and a ComplexSSM + causal self-attention temporal stack
Transformer (11L depth recurrence with 10 unique BlockCores, LeakyReLU(0.5)² MLP)
11L/512d/8H/4KV/3xMLP (relu²), U-Net skip, Partial RoPE (16/64), XSA last 4, BigramHash(2048), VE128 on layers 9-10, SmearGate
11L/512d/8H/4KV/3xMLP (relu²) with U-Net skip connections, Partial RoPE (16/64), XSA last 4 layers, BigramHash(2048), VE128 on layers 9-10, SmearGate, logit softcap 30, tied embeddings
Transformer (custom with LeakyReLU, U-Net skip connections, Grouped Query Attention, partial RoPE, Cross-Self Attention, BigramHash embedding, RMSNorm, parameter banking)
mixed int6 quantization with fp16 tied embedding passthrough and STE QAT
int6 post-training quantization with selective fp16 preservation
Muon-aware QAT (STE or Gaussian noise), targeting int8/int6 quantization sensitivity
FP16 embeddings passthrough with int8/tied-embedding export implied
mixed selective precision with fp16 tied embedding and late-K passthrough
Muon (hidden/attn) + Adam (embeddings/scalars) for training; SGD with momentum=0.9 for TTT
Muon (lr=0.025, WD=0.04, momentum=0.99) for base training; SGD + momentum 0.9 for TTT
